title: Landslide warning signs and lateral escape
summary: A recognition-and-escape card for cracking ground, tilting trees, new water flow, rumbling, or moving debris, with a priority on leaving the slide path rather than observing it.
content:
A recognition-and-escape card for cracking ground, tilting trees, new water flow, rumbling, or moving debris, with a priority on leaving the slide path rather than observing it.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Treat sudden cracks, leaning poles or trees, doors that jam, new springs, falling stones, or a growing rumble as urgent warning signs.
Move immediately away from the expected flow path and toward higher, stable ground; when possible move sideways out of a channel rather than following it.
Warn nearby people without delaying your own escape and call 112 from a safe location with the road, slope, stream, and direction of movement.
Stay away from the affected slope and downstream channels because additional movement can follow rain or aftershocks.
warnings: Do not cross an active debris flow on foot or by vehicle.
A quiet slope is not automatically stable after the first movement.
common_mistakes: Starting Landslide warning signs and lateral escape without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Landslide warning signs and lateral escape.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Landslide warning signs and lateral escape when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Landslide warning signs and lateral escape when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Landslide warning signs and lateral escape.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Landslide warning signs and lateral escape safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Landslide warning signs and lateral escape when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Landslide warning signs and lateral escape.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Landslide warning signs and lateral escape should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Landslide warning signs and lateral escape whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Landslide warning signs and lateral escape in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Landslide warning signs and lateral escape after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Landslide warning signs and lateral escape cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Landslide warning signs and lateral escape are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Landslide warning signs and lateral escape to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Landslide warning signs and lateral escape.']
